title: oeis_live + gw_observatory bridge OOM resolution via shell-side payload trim

trigger.summary: after dockerfile curl patch landed 14/16 bridges PASS, oeis (rc=137 SIGKILL) and gw (timeout/OOM) remained as the 2 holdouts; both blocked by 4GB cgroup OOM during interp text-walk on multi-MB payloads

parser_hot_path.oeis: parse_b_file does text.split("\n") then per-line raw.substring/raw.index_of in a while loop; on 1.2MB / 120K lines the interp string allocator pressure spikes well past the 4GB cgroup cap (oom_kill=4 in /sys/fs/cgroup/memory.events)
parser_hot_path.gw: extract_event_keys + _find_event_anchor + _extract_field_after each do per-byte text.substring(j, j+1) walks across the full 2.74MB string; for 368 events × 5 fields × 60K-char span scan = O(10^8) substring allocs, blowing the 4GB cap

diagnose.container_cap_reaffirmed: cpus=4 / mem=4g / memory-swap=4g per project_mac_hard_landing_policy 2026-04-25 — NOT changed (canonical Linux equivalent of darwin no-cgroup state)
diagnose.root_cause_one_liner: interp's per-byte string scanning via .substring(j, j+1) is quadratic-ish in total bytes allocated; 1MB+ inputs blow the 4GB cap

fix_chosen.option: OPT-A — shell-side payload trim BEFORE bytes reach interp

rejected_options.OPT-B_streaming_parser: would require interp string-stream API not present; large refactor outside raw 73 admissibility (surgical-only)
rejected_options.OPT-C_hardcoded_fallback: would lose live-fetch semantics; bridges would silently degrade to static data when upstream changes (raw 71 risk: bridge claim drift unnoticed). reserved for true network failure path.

fix_chosen.rationale: shell pipe runs in the same docker container, completes in <1s, and reduces oeis 1.2MB→~2KB (head -n 100, only first 6 anchor terms needed) and gw 2.74MB→~60KB (grep -E '"commonName"|"GPS":|"mass_1_source"|"mass_2_source"|"luminosity_distance"', strips strain[] which is 95%+ of bytes). live semantics PRESERVED — real OEIS / GWOSC servers, real bytes, just bounded.

oeis_diff_summary.fetch_b_file: added ' | head -n 100' to curl pipeline — caps selftest fetch at first 100 lines (~2KB regardless of upstream size)
oeis_diff_summary.fetch_sequence_full: new function for --seq mode — uses 'head -c 200000' (200KB cap, ~13K terms — safe upper bound for any practical use)
oeis_diff_summary.run_single_callsite: switched from fetch_sequence to fetch_sequence_full for --seq mode
oeis_diff_summary.comment_block: +13 lines explaining ω-cycle, OOM signature, fix choice; reason+fix trailer (raw 66 ai-native-error)

gw_diff_summary.fetch_catalog: added ' | grep -E ...' filter to curl pipeline — keeps only commonName/GPS/mass_1_source/mass_2_source/luminosity_distance lines, drops strain[] tuples (97% of bytes)
gw_diff_summary.comment_block: +15 lines explaining ω-cycle, OOM signature, parser tolerance to brace-less filtered text; reason+fix trailer (raw 66)

patch.raw_73_admissibility: PASS — only added 1 grep stage to gw curl, 1 head stage to oeis curl, 1 helper fn, 1 callsite swap; no parser logic modified, no JSON parser written, no interp change
patch.raw_66_ai_native_error: PASS — both fetch fn comment blocks include explicit reason+fix trailer (cgroup OOM signature + shell-trim fix)

before.oeis_live: FAIL rc=137 (SIGKILL OOM during parse_b_file on 1.2MB sigma sequence)
before.gw_observatory: FAIL timeout or OOM (no stdout produced before 90s wall, container oom_kill counter increments)

after.oeis_live: PASS — sentinel: __OEIS_LIVE_BRIDGE__ PASS A000396_first=6 sigma6=12 tau6=4 phi6=2 axis_oeis_consistent=true (cross-check vs hexa_sim_verify_grid axis_oeis static table OK)
after.gw_observatory: PASS — sentinel: __GW_BRIDGE__ PASS events_total=263 latest_event=GW250114_082203 first_event=GW150914 (anchor present, count above >=90 threshold)

full_bridge_health.scope: all 16 bridges sequentially via gtimeout 90 hexa run tool/<X>_bridge.hexa --selftest

verify.data_quirk_noted: GW150914's first catalog entry (O1_O2-Preliminary v1) lacks mass_1_source/mass_2_source/luminosity_distance fields entirely — they are not present in upstream JSON. brief shows 'm1=null m2=null d_L=null'. NOT a fix-induced regression; same data quirk would have appeared in original 2.7MB parse path. event count + anchor presence (selftest's actual gates) both pass.

before_after.duration_delta_explanation: 47s vs 22s — slower because oeis + gw now actually run end-to-end (previously they died fast; now they fetch + trim + parse + emit). sustainable for ω-cycle cadence.

tier_1: oeis_live_bridge OOM resolved — head -n 100 cap on b-file fetch, full live semantics preserved, all 4 OEIS sequences cross-check against axis_oeis static table
gw_observatory_bridge OOM resolved — grep -E 5-field filter strips 97% of jsonfull payload, 263 events parsed, GW150914 anchor present, n=6 SE(3) DOF binding intact
bridge health: 14/16 PASS → 16/16 PASS (100%); zero remaining live-fetch blockers in registry
tier_2: atlas_health_timeline.jsonl appended with new entry (16/16 PASS, fix attribution)
shell-side trim is now a reusable pattern for any future bridge whose upstream payload exceeds ~500KB — document in next-cycle review of bridge-tool authoring guide
underlying interp text.substring O(n) hot path remains — flagged but out-of-scope for this surgical cycle (would require interp string-slice optimization or bytestring API)

raw_admissibility.raw_73_minimum_add: PASS — 2 shell pipeline stages added, 1 helper fn, 1 callsite swap; zero parser logic change, zero interp change, zero container cap change
raw_admissibility.raw_66_ai_native_error: PASS — fetch fn comment blocks document reason (4GB cgroup OOM signature) + fix (shell-side trim) trailer; consumer error messages unchanged
raw_admissibility.raw_71_no_premature_retire: PASS — bridge claims (OEIS perfect-number anchor, GW150914 anchor) UNCHANGED; live fetch path UNCHANGED in semantics, just bounded in size; if upstream data ever moves we'll see real fetch-empty failures, not silent fallback masking
raw_admissibility.project_mac_hard_landing_policy: PASS — container cpus=4/mem=4g cap UNCHANGED; problem solved at the bridge layer, not by raising the cap
